babyDevelopment: The baby's sweat glands are starting to develop. Fat (adipose tissue) is beginning to accumulate under the skin, helping with warmth and metabolism. The umbilical cord is growing thicker and more resilient.
bodyChanges: Your center of gravity is shifting as your bump grows. You might experience occasional 'dizzy spells' if you stand up too quickly. Some women notice increased vaginal discharge (leukorrhea), which is normal as long as it's clear or white and odorless.
tips: Incorporate more calcium for baby's bone development
Move slowly when standing up to avoid dizziness
Consider a prenatal massage for any back discomfort

do: Eat plenty of fiber and stay hydrated
Practice deep breathing to help with relaxation
dont: Lift heavy objects that put strain on your back
Skip your regular blood pressure checks
Ignore any unusual or persistent headaches

travelAdvisory.note: Keep your travel plans active and stay hydrated.

warningSignsToWatch: Sudden swelling in face or hands
milestoneDescription: Baby is beginning to store fat for energy and warmth after birth.
